entries.name: New Website
entries.description: The desire to talk about more things than just Metroid, wanting to host more different types of projects and a growing need for newer features lead me to completely rewrite the website.

entries.name: Website Launch
entries.description: Launched ConConner's Crazy Content with all of my Metroid hacks and tools. My content was intially only hosted on a different website. After a lack of backups almost erased my projects from the internet, I decided to create my own hosting site.

entries.name: Learning C#
entries.description: During development of ConCon's MapMapper I quickly realised how limiting my current tech stack was. Gamemaker Studio might prove as a good engine to make games, but it is very bad for Programs with a complex UI. Therefore, I started learning C# with Windows Forms, a framework actually designed for Desktop Applications.

entries.name: Programming with Gamemaker Studio
entries.description: Before I only ever used visual programming software, since I was scared to actually learn coding. Since my primary focus was always on making games, I decided to give programming with Gamemaker Studio a shot. While none of my prototypes ever really got finished, Gamemaker has taught me everything I needed to know to start programming.

entries.name: First Steps in ROM-Hacking
entries.description:
I used to dream about making my own worlds in the style of the Metroid series. In late 2017 I discovered a tool that streamlined the creation of these worlds by directly modifying the games Metroid: Zero Mission and Metroid: Fusion.
The picture is from my first room ever created.
